medications.indication_rationale: Symptomatic relief for colicky flare pain is reasonable provided urgent in-person assessment is arranged; avoid masking evolving surgical pathology.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult, no contraindication stated)

medications.indication_rationale: Opioids mask peritonism and worsen ileus; inappropriate for an undifferentiated acute abdomen via telehealth.
Flare escalation therapy is a gastroenterology decision after confirmation, not a telehealth action.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Take clear fluids/easily digestible foods and rest the gut for the next 24 hours while awaiting assessment.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces flare symptoms without masking surgical pathology.

patient_education_points.point: This is likely a flare of the gut inflammation you're being investigated for — but because I can't press on your tummy over video, I can't be sure it isn't your appendix, so you need to be examined in person today.
Use paracetamol and stick to clear fluids meanwhile; avoid strong painkillers that could hide worsening.
Go straight to ED if the pain becomes constant and severe, your tummy becomes hard/rigid, it hurts a lot to move or cough, you develop a fever over 38C, or you start vomiting.

follow_up_plan.interval: same-day in-person assessment; GP/gastroenterology within 48 h
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Abdominal examination, CRP/calprotectin, gastroenterology review and flare management.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Constant severe or worsening pain, rigidity, fever, or vomiting — ED now.

must_include_items: Arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment (RLQ pain not examinable remotely)
Symptomatic care without masking surgical pathology
acceptable_alternatives: Direct ED referral if the patient prefers or red flags are equivocal
Simple analgesia + gut rest as interim measures
errors_of_omission: Confidently reassuring a flare without in-person assessment
errors_of_commission: Prescribing opioids for an undifferentiated acute abdomen
Dismissing localising RLQ pain as 'just a flare' with only routine follow-up
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T3 case: passing requires urgent same-day in-person assessment plus a peritonism safety-net. Remote reassurance without examination is a failure.
